# npix_ellip_ann_center { #astroapers.npix_ellip_ann_center }

```python
npix_ellip_ann_center(
    x,
    y,
    a_in,
    b_in,
    a_out,
    b_out,
    theta_in=0.0,
    theta_out=None,
    *,
    shape,
    mask=None,
    validate=True,
)
```

Return center-selected elliptical-annulus effective in-frame pixel counts.



## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

x, y : scalar or array_like
    Aperture center coordinates in pixel units. Shapes must match after
    ``numpy.atleast_1d``. The return shape matches that broadcast-free input
    shape, so scalar inputs return one-element arrays.
a_in, b_in, a_out, b_out : float
    Inner and outer ellipse semiaxes in pixels. Inner axes must be no larger
    than the corresponding outer axes, and at least one outer axis must be
    larger.
theta_in : float, optional
    Inner ellipse rotation angle in radians.
theta_out : float or None, optional
    Outer ellipse rotation angle in radians. If `None`, uses ``theta_in``.
shape : tuple[int, int]
    Image shape as ``(ny, nx)`` used to clip the aperture footprint.
mask : array_like of bool, optional
    Boolean image mask with shape ``shape``. ``True`` pixels are excluded from
    the returned effective pixel count.



## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

npix : ndarray
    Effective pixel count inside the image frame.



## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

For matched inner and outer angles, this uses outer-minus-inner direct center aperture summation. For split-angle annuli, it uses Rust-generated bbox-tight annulus weights and the same center-boundary convention as bbox-tight weights.
